## Houston 0.4.10

The Windows-on-ARM + Settings cleanup release. Houston now ships a native ARM64 MSI alongside the x64 build, so Surface and Snapdragon laptops run the engine without emulation. Settings collapses from a long list into 3 focused sections plus an in-app Report bug flow that opens a Linear ticket for you. The provider picker is rebuilt around the same integration cards used elsewhere, and a stack of three codex regressions (silent freezes, "A local tool failed to start", title summaries that crashed) are fixed.

### What's new

- **Native Windows-on-ARM.** Houston now ships an ARM64 MSI in addition to the x64 one. Composio is bundled as a real aarch64 binary (selected at runtime via `IsWow64Process2`), PortableGit ships per arch and auto-extracts on first launch, and `CLAUDE_CODE_GIT_BASH_PATH` is exported globally at engine boot so every Claude Code spawn site inherits it. No more "install Git for Windows" hand-holding; no more emulated x64.
- **Settings: 3 sections + Report bug.** The settings page collapses to **Workspace** (name, language, timezone, theme all in one), **AI provider**, and **Connect phone**. A new **Report bug** entry submits straight into the same Linear pipeline the red error toast uses, and the success toast surfaces the returned issue ID so you can reference it in support DMs.
- **Provider picker is now integration-card style.** Single-click cards, one action per state. Disconnected → opens the browser sign-in. Connected → confirms and signs out. Coming-soon providers get a pill instead of a clickable action. Workspace default is set silently when a provider connects (last-connected wins); per-mission model selection in the chat selector is the primary UX for switching.
- **Trimmed model lineup.** Anthropic: Opus + Sonnet (default Sonnet); Haiku removed from picker. OpenAI: a single GPT-5.5 (default); mini and codex variants removed. The flagships are the right default for a general agent that does email, planning, and varied tool use, rather than the coding-specialised tiers.
- **Per-mission model selection now persists.** Picking a different model on one mission no longer cascades to the others. Each mission stores its own provider + model in its `activity.json`, and switching providers in the chat selector now surfaces a toast if the persist fails instead of silently dropping the choice.
- **In-app legal pages.** New Privacy Policy and Terms of Service pages on the marketing site, linked from every footer.

### Reliability

- **Codex CLI: three stacked regressions fixed.**
  - **No more "A local tool failed to start" from a stale global config.** Users with a newer codex CLI on their machine sometimes had `xhigh` written to `~/.codex/config.toml`. Houston used to spawn codex through the shell PATH, which often resolved to an older copy that rejects `xhigh` and exited 1 before producing any output. The engine now always passes `-c model_reasoning_effort="<value>"` (defaulting to `medium`) so a stale global config can never kill a session.
  - **Bundled codex bumped to 0.130.0** (was 0.121.0), which the API requires for `gpt-5.5`. In dev builds the engine now resolves codex from the staged binary under `app/src-tauri/resources/bin/` instead of whatever's on PATH.
  - **No more 5 to 10 second "frozen" feeling at the start of a session.** Codex 0.130 in `--json` mode emits `turn.started` then stays silent for several seconds while the model reasons internally. The parser used to drop that event, so the chat panel had nothing to anchor "Mission in progress…" on. `turn.started` now seeds a streaming-reasoning placeholder, and the first real output, `turn.completed`, or `turn.failed` closes it.
- **Cryptic Windows NTSTATUS exit codes decoded.** When a launch fails with `0xc000001d`, `0xc0000135`, or `0xc0000139`, you now see actionable text instead of the raw hex.
- **Engine subprocess console window suppressed on Windows.** No more flashing black box on first launch.
- **Engine writes to `%USERPROFILE%\.houston\`** instead of wherever the app happened to be launched from. Fixes a long-standing path bug in the Windows port.
- **Engine stderr is captured to `engine.log.<date>`** instead of inherited into `NUL`. Bug reports now include real engine output.
- **Tunnel `/allocate` honors `Retry-After` on 429s.** Mobile pairing retries no longer hammer the relay.

### Before you upgrade

- **Mac:** quit Houston before installing. macOS does not always replace a running app cleanly. If in doubt, remove `/Applications/Houston.app` and drag the new copy in.
- **Windows x64:** auto-update from 0.4.7 or later should pull you forward automatically. The MSI is still not OS-code-signed (SignPath is still pending). One SmartScreen "Run anyway" prompt on first install only.
- **Windows ARM64:** this is the first ARM64 build. There is no auto-update path from an emulated x64 install. Download the ARM64 MSI manually, uninstall the x64 build first, then install the ARM64 one. Future ARM64 → ARM64 updates apply automatically.

### If you are on 0.4.5 or later (Mac / Windows x64)

Houston should detect 0.4.10, download it, install it, and relaunch into the new version on its own.

### If you are on 0.4.2 or earlier (Mac)

Auto-update will not pull you forward from those builds. Quit Houston, drag a fresh DMG over `/Applications/Houston.app`, and reopen. After that one manual step you are back on the healthy updater path and future versions apply automatically.

### Known limitations

- **Windows MSI not yet OS-code-signed.** SignPath integration still pending. One SmartScreen "Run anyway" click on first install only, both x64 and ARM64.
- **"Continue with Microsoft" sign-in for Houston itself remains hidden** while we reconcile the Supabase Azure provider with the production callback URL. Google sign-in is unaffected. The Microsoft 365 + Outlook tutorial path is unaffected.
- **Mobile companion is still beta.** Expect occasional reconnect hiccups. Keep your Mac awake while using it.
